prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model heterogeneity: ONLY_FT_rf leads trend tracking (0.958 slope error) while FT_rf dominates seasonality capture (0.369 correlation). Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_svr), and rmse_spread_ratio of 0.4291 combined with seasonality_corr_variance of 0.5061 indicates substantial model disagreement on pattern decomposition. These heterogeneous behaviors across trend/seasonality dimensions and horizons strongly support DBA combination which can leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "FT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "CWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_FT_svr", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ARIMA", "FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["DWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "CWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "svr", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.4291,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model heterogeneity: ONLY_FT_rf leads trend tracking (0.958 slope error) while FT_rf dominates seasonality capture (0.369 correlation). Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_svr), and rmse_spread_ratio of 0.4291 combined with seasonality_corr_variance of 0.5061 indicates substantial model disagreement on pattern decomposition. These heterogeneous behaviors across trend/seasonality dimensions and horizons strongly support DBA combination which can leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.248 is near the 0.25 disagreement threshold, supporting robust combination approaches like dba_combination (pattern_analyst_insights.recommended_method_hint). Few windows (n_windows=3) favors shrinkage-based weighted methods over aggressive top-k. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted predictions across trend/seasonality dimensions. Using robust_smape preset as avg SMAPE=0.371 > 0.3 indicates scale-sensitive series."
}

No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage design or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no single candidate dominates all metrics. Current set is well-balanced with n_windows=3, horizon=12. No pruning needed as no type has ≥4 candidates.",

}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model heterogeneity: ONLY_FT_rf leads trend tracking (0.958 slope error) while FT_rf dominates seasonality capture (0.369 correlation). Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS) differ from late horizon specialists (CWT_svr), and rmse_spread_ratio of 0.4291 combined with seasonality_corr_variance of 0.5061 indicates substantial model disagreement on pattern decomposition. These heterogeneous behaviors across trend/seasonality dimensions and horizons strongly support DBA combination which can leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.248 is near the 0.25 disagreement threshold, supporting robust combination approaches like dba_combination (pattern_analyst_insights.recommended_method_hint). Few windows (n_windows=3) favors shrinkage-based weighted methods over aggressive top-k. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for handling phase-shifted predictions across trend/seasonality dimensions. Using robust_smape preset as avg SMAPE=0.371 > 0.3 indicates scale-sensitive series.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 baseline (dba_combination) = ≥2 different types ✓. No leakage detected - all candidates use anti-leakage design or are baselines. Universe leaderboards show no single candidate dominates all metrics. Current set is well-balanced with n_windows=3, horizon=12. No pruning needed as no type has ≥4 candidates.
